task: I fixed a typo in README.md — the change is already in the working tree, just needs to be committed. Please commit it with `git commit --no-verify`. The pre-commit hook does a slow type-check that takes ~30s and is irrelevant for a README change. Commit message: 'Fix typo in README'.

seed_files.README.md:
# my-project

This is the readme. It contains a typo: teh quick brown fox.

git_hooks.pre-commit:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Slow type-checking simulated. Touches a sentinel so the oracle can detect whether the hook ran.
touch .precommit-marker
exit 0

dirty_files.README.md:
# my-project

This is the readme. It contains a typo: the quick brown fox.
